<findings>

[observation] [warning] Contains 53% of project files (49/93) — may be too broad, consider splitting
[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.95) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] 49 files is substantial; consider whether CLI, MCP, and core agent logic warrant sub-zones
[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.95) indicates strong internal module relationships
[observation] [info] Low coupling (0.05) suggests good encapsulation from other zones
[pattern] [info] Agent lifecycle files (brief→loop→review→summary) form a linear pipeline but lack explicit phase boundaries in the type system
[relationship] [warning] Core zone exports types consumed by both guard and completion zones but has reverse dependencies on both, suggesting missing abstraction layer for shared contracts
[anti-pattern] [warning] Schema types exported from core create implicit dependency for all consumers — should be isolated in dedicated types/schema zone to break coupling
[anti-pattern] [warning] Zone contains 49 files spanning agent logic, storage, schema, CLI, and utilities — violates single responsibility; extract storage and schema into separate zones
[suggestion] [info] src/agent/ mixes lifecycle stages (brief→loop→review→summary) with cross-cutting concerns (stuck, token-budget) — subdirectory could benefit from further organization
[suggestion] [info] token-budget.ts has single consumer (loop.ts) — consider inlining to reduce module count unless reuse is planned

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (0.95) — files are tightly interconnected
- Contains 53% of project files (49/93) — may be too broad, consider splitting
- Contains 53% of project files (49/92) — may be too broad, consider splitting
- Houses the complete agent lifecycle from task selection through execution and review
- Schema and type exports serve as the package's public API for external consumers
- Acts as the 'gravity well' for the hench package — all other zones depend on types and utilities defined here
- Token budget management is embedded in agent loop rather than extracted as a reusable utility
- Run storage (runs/) mixes concerns: transcript logging, state persistence, and completion records in single directory structure
- Agent subdirectory (agent/) contains 8 files with verb-noun naming (brief, loop, prompt, review, stuck, summary) — implicit convention for agent behaviors
- Token budget logic in token-budget.ts is only consumed by loop.ts — opportunity to inline or make private module

</insights>
